title: coris monspeliensis subsp. monspeliensis
intro: Coris monspeliensis subsp. monspeliensis, also known as the Provence Bugloss or Bugloss of Provence, is a perennial plant species in the Boraginaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments due to its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits associated with Coris monspeliensis subsp. monspeliensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, with its dried leaves and roots being used in various forms of folk medicine.
